title: Red Group PASS: m=9 cosmos orbit period=24 (ord(F) in GL₂(Z/9Z))
_fixture_purpose: Anchor fixture. The QA Fibonacci shift F=[[0,1],[1,1]] has order 24 in GL₂(Z/9Z): F^24≡I mod 9 and F^12=[[8,0],[0,8]]=-I≢I (so 24 is minimal). The affine QA map (b',e')→(e',b'+e'+1) mod 9 also has period 24 because the translation sum vanishes: (I+F+...+F^23)=(I+F+...+F^11)(I+F^12)=(I+F+...+F^11)·0=0. This matches the QA cosmos orbit period for m=9. det(F)=-1=red norm N_red(φ)=φ·ψ=−1 (φ=(1+√5)/2 is a red unit of norm −1 in Z[√5]).

red_group.T_matrix_derivation: Fibonacci shift: (b,e) → (e, b+e mod m); matrix of φ-multiplication in Z[√5]/mZ[√5] (red chromatic isometry group)
red_group._char_poly_note: roots are φ=(1+√5)/2 and ψ=(1−√5)/2; φ·ψ = det = −1 (red norm)

_period_witnesses._note: F^12 = −I mod 9 ≠ I, confirming 24 is minimal; F^24 = I ✓

wildberger_reference.theorem: 1D Metrical Geometry, Red Isometry Group (Wildberger arXiv:math/0701338)
wildberger_reference.statement: Three chromatic isometry groups on P¹(F): Blue=[ac−bd:ad+bc], Red=[ac+bd:ad+bc], Green=[ac:bd]. For split-complex ring Z[√D], red isometry matrix [[u,Dv],[v,u]] with u²−Dv²=unit. For D=5 and φ=(1+√5)/2: N_red(φ)=−1 (norm −1 unit).
wildberger_reference.qa_form: QA T-operator = Fibonacci shift F=[[0,1],[1,1]] = red isometry by φ in Z[√5]/mZ[√5]. Cosmos orbit period = ord(F) in GL₂(Z/mZ).

validation_checks.description: det(T_matrix) ≡ −1 mod m (red norm of φ)
orbit_period is minimal (T^(P/k) ≢ I for prime k|P)
orbit_type ∈ valid set AND period=1 ↔ singularity
